comment_content: Giorgi has been fine and made no mistakes, Claiming Allison is the reason we were winning before his injury is bonkers. The Setup and Tactics are terrible. We had been very lucky and skating on thin ice since the PSG game last season. It looks like the players have lost faith in The Manager and his methods, his choice of personnel and lack of adaptability and creativity in overcoming the various systems and teams we have struggled against. Repeating the same mistakes over and over and expecting a different outcome is madness. We are either incredibly passive or are playing MadBall 3-2-5 with 8 strikers on the pitch. Slots tactics and methods are not getting the best from the players we have, not learning from mistakes. Its unacceptable and maybe the reason was simply last year we had Jonny Heitinga as a coach. I dont know but imho the players have lost faith and Arne has lost the dressing room. I cannot see how this will change with the current manager and staff. Imo another loss or bad performance will be the the Coup de Grace. And we only have Real Madrid who will test us to the max coming soon and hopefully it will be a turning point for the positive.

comment_content:
Simply put, Liverpool of last season still had Klopp’s DNA of heavy metal pressing imprinted on it, players pressing relentlessly as second nature, so Slot’s more patient, control style of football complimented the system well, leading to more clean sheets. However, as the season wore on, around Feb/Match, you could see Klopp’s DNA being coached out, culminating in the lifeless performance in the Carabao Cup Final. 

This season, the heavy metal pressing had been completely coached out of the team, in its place is a slow build up and a lack of emphasis on winning second balls. 

This unfortunately is on Slot. It’s clear as day. The question now is, do Liverpool fans see what Slot is aiming for in the performances? No. Do they enjoy this style of football? No. Are teams intimidated by playing Liverpool? No. 

This will not get better. An axe needs wielding. Most Anfield regulars know it, we’ve known it since Spring.

comment_content:
I don't get what is happening with Liverpool. Yes, sure, they looked a little rusty in the first couple of league rounds, but you still saw that there was a winning team waiting to burst into form...but somehow, things have just sort of stopped...and now (although, things can still change quickly, as there are alot of game weeks left), it seems that it won't be that close title race between pretty much only LFC and my own Arsenal...I had NO doubts before the season that LFC would be the team to beat.

I'm not here to rub it in or gloat, as (as a matter of fact) I have always liked Liverpool, and am not afraid nor ashamed to say that. (Heck, even our captain comes from a Liverpool-family! :D) 

And I would much rather have that knife-edge close, all the way to the last round, battle for the title with Liverpool than with City, which seems to be getting back into their form. (Only thing I like about that, as a Norwegian, is Braut Haaland, and him being an unstoppable force of nature at the moment...geez, what a striker! Forget Isak, forget Gyökeres...there's nobody in the Premiership like him)

So, I hope Slot and the guys turns things around.

Still, THIS is the year it comes home to North London. 

COYG!

comment_content:
I don’t think Slot’s system fits Liverpool’s DNA. Klopp had already evolved the team beautifully — players like Wirtz, Isak, Frimpong, and Kerkez were tailor-made for that fast, aggressive style.
Now they’re trapped in a slow, positional setup that kills their instincts.
You can’t replace Liverpool’s soul with Dutch total football — we’re built on intensity, emotion, and controlled chaos.
If Slot doesn’t blend his ideas with the club’s identity, we could lose not just matches, but what makes this team Liverpool.

comment_content:
Liverpool fans,
Our performance’s been dropping game after game. It’s not down to a lack of talent, it’s because we’ve no proper, solid midfield anymore – nothing like the days of Fabinho and Henderson. The engine room just isn’t there.

And another thing — we’ve got two wingers who are basically doing the exact same thing every match. Honestly, Gakpo and Salah shouldn’t both be starting. They just end up making the same runs, same moves, same outcome.

Endo’s the only midfielder we’ve got who actually puts in a proper defensive shift, and he’s sat on the bench. The only lad who looks like he could bring something different up front is young Rio – and even at his age, he deserves more minutes by now.

This side’s lost that fighting spirit — the one we used to see in lads like Henderson, Van den Berg, and Kelleher. In short, with this starting eleven, we’re going nowhere fast.

comment_content: Guys stop blaming Salah, he is not THE PROBLEM.... Clearly it is the manager's fault to play the players not in their right positions. See when Salah changed his position, he was able to get a fantastic goal. Also, same with Dom, his chemistry with Salah is on another level, yet the manager doesn't want to change the formation and put the players in their right positions where they can show their qualities in. Same problem with Wirtz, he is not good at that position at all so we can't see his qualities. There was no defense from our side to the point that made our attackers like Salah, Cody, Dom, Hugo and Chiesa defend instead of attacking. Hugo was nowhere to be found today, I even forgot that he was playing today. Macca, Wirtz and Chiesa made horrible mistakes today as well that cost us a lot. Also, clearly that our goalkeeper isn't good like Alisson so there were many factors why we lost and why we keep losing but people choose to ignore all of that and just stick to blame Salah instead because somehow it's his fault, he should play all the roles of the 11 players I guess then. People forgot or maybe got used on that he did everything and carried the whole team last season alone and won the premier league so he should do everything all alone as if it is an individual sport not a team sport. Instead of blaming Salah, blame the manager because all of these losses and dropping performances from the players clearly his fault not a player's fault.
